examples.title: Peterson's Algorithm for Two Processes
examples.description: Software-based mutual exclusion using shared variables without hardware instructions
examples.code:
SHARED VARIABLES:
  flag[2] : ARRAY[0..1] OF BOOLEAN
  turn : INTEGER

PROCESS P0:
WHILE TRUE DO:
  flag[0] ← TRUE
  turn ← 1
  WHILE (flag[1] AND turn = 1) DO:
    // busy wait
  END WHILE
  // CRITICAL SECTION
  flag[0] ← FALSE
  // REMAINDER SECTION
END WHILE

PROCESS P1:
WHILE TRUE DO:
  flag[1] ← TRUE
  turn ← 0
  WHILE (flag[0] AND turn = 0) DO:
    // busy wait
  END WHILE
  // CRITICAL SECTION
  flag[1] ← FALSE
  // REMAINDER SECTION
END WHILE
examples.explanation: Each process first declares intention to enter by setting its flag to TRUE. Then it gives priority to the other process by setting turn. The while loop busy-waits until the other process does not want to enter OR it is the other process's turn. After exiting critical section, flag is reset to FALSE.

examples.title: Semaphore Implementation
examples.description: Implementation of wait and signal operations on semaphores using Python threading
examples.code:
import threading

class Semaphore:
    def __init__(self, value=1):
        self.value = value
        self.lock = threading.Lock()
        self.queue = []
    
    def wait(self):
        self.lock.acquire()
        self.value -= 1
        if self.value < 0:
            # Add current thread to waiting queue
            self.queue.append(threading.current_thread())
            self.lock.release()
            # Block the thread
            thread = self.queue.pop(0)
            thread.join()  # Wait until signaled
        else:
            self.lock.release()
    
    def signal(self):
        self.lock.acquire()
        self.value += 1
        if self.value <= 0:
            # Wake up one waiting thread
            # In practice, use proper thread synchronization
            pass
        self.lock.release()

# Usage for mutual exclusion
mutex = Semaphore(1)

def critical_section():
    mutex.wait()
    # Protected code here
    mutex.signal()
examples.explanation: This shows a basic semaphore implementation. The wait() operation decrements the value and blocks if negative. The signal() operation increments and wakes a waiting process. In practice, Python's threading module provides Lock and Condition for this purpose.

examples.title: Test-and-Set Lock Implementation
examples.description: Implementing mutual exclusion using atomic Test-and-Set instruction in C
examples.explanation: atomic_flag_test_and_set() is the C11 atomic equivalent of Test-and-Set. It atomically sets the flag to true and returns its previous value. The lock function busy-waits (spins) until the lock is acquired. This is efficient for short critical sections in multiprocessor systems.

examples.title: Producer-Consumer Problem Solution
examples.description: Solution to bounded buffer producer-consumer problem using semaphores in C
examples.explanation: Three semaphores control the solution: empty (initialized to BUFFER_SIZE) counts empty slots, full (initialized to 0) counts filled slots, and mutex (initialized to 1) provides mutual exclusion. The producer waits for an empty slot, adds item, signals a full slot. The consumer waits for a full slot, removes item, signals an empty slot.
